strategic_profile.executive_summary: Illinois Institute of Technology (Illinois Tech) is a private, mid-sized research university located in Chicago, Illinois. Known for its rigorous STEM, architecture, and design programs, the university strongly emphasizes career readiness and hands-on learning through its Elevate program and Interprofessional Projects (IPRO) curriculum.

analyst_takeaways.insight: All undergraduates must complete two Interprofessional Projects (IPRO), emphasizing team-based, cross-disciplinary problem solving.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students build tangible portfolios and real-world collaboration skills before graduation, making them highly employable.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The social environment is heavily academically focused and relatively quiet, with limited traditional party culture.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Best suited for self-driven, introverted, or intensely focused students who prioritize studies and professional development over a bustling Greek life.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Illinois Tech offers exceptionally generous merit scholarships and meets a high percentage of need, mitigating its high sticker price.
analyst_takeaways.implication: High-achieving applicants (especially those applying Early Decision/Action) have strong chances of receiving full-tuition or heavily subsidized aid packages.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Quiet and heavily academically focused. Clubs center around major-related interests, tech, and professional development. The Greek life exists but does not dominate the campus culture.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Situated in the Bronzeville neighborhood just minutes from downtown Chicago, students have excellent access to public transit (CTA), providing a major advantage for securing internships and city exploration.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Research is heavily encouraged starting in the freshman year. The Elevate program ensures that every student has guaranteed access to hands-on experiences, research opportunities, or internships.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test optional. Submission of SAT or ACT scores is not required for admission but will be considered if submitted.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist data not publicly disclosed. Yield rate calculated based on full-time freshman enrollment vs admits.
Data aggregated from 2022-2023 Common Data Set snippet.

gpa.notes: The average unweighted GPA is frequently cited around 3.98. Rigor in STEM classes (AP Calculus, AP Physics) is heavily prioritized.

testing.policy_note: 30% of applicants submitted SAT scores and 8% submitted ACT scores.

colleges.name: College of Computing
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students with strong backgrounds in AP Calculus, AP Computer Science, and logical problem solving. Suitable for those targeting high-ROI software engineering or data science careers.
colleges.housing_profile: Standard campus housing; most freshmen live in McCormick Student Village or State Street Village.

majors.name: Computer Science
notable_professors: Dr. Mustafa Bilgic (Machine Learning, Active Learning)
majors.notes: Accredited by the Computing Accreditation Commission of ABET.

colleges.name: Armour College of Engineering
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students applying should showcase strong grades in high-level math and physical sciences.

majors.name: Mechanical Engineering
majors.notes: Accredited by the Engineering Accreditation Commission of ABET.

colleges.name: College of Architecture
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for creatively and technically balanced students looking for a studio-driven, rigorous 5-year B.Arch program.
colleges.student_archetype: Creative designer with strong spatial reasoning.

majors.name: Architecture
majors.special_requirements: Portfolio review may be required for specific scholarships like the Crown Scholarship.
majors.notes: A 5-year professional degree heavily influenced by its former director, Ludwig Mies van der Rohe.

application_deadlines.notes: Applying ED provides a significant statistical advantage (estimated ~65% acceptance rate) and consideration for full-tuition scholarships.

supplemental_requirements.details: A portfolio of 4-20 pieces of creative work, including at least one freehand drawing, is required for Crown Scholarship consideration.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Strong emphasis is placed on readiness for a technical curriculum. Advanced math and science preparation heavily influences decisions.

major_selection_tactics: Apply Early Decision or Early Action to significantly boost admission odds (ED rates approach 65% vs RD near 52%).
Align high school coursework rigorously with the desired major; e.g., calculus-based physics and object-oriented programming for CS or Engineering.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Applying to interdisciplinary or less impacted 'Tech+' degrees (like Computer Information Systems) can be an effective alternative if the applicant's profile isn't quite strong enough for core Computer Science.

financials.tuition_model: Flat rate tuition for all students (no distinction between in-state and out-of-state).

scholarships.name: Crown Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Covers full tuition for five years of study in Architecture.

scholarships.name: Illinois Tech Purpose
scholarships.benefits: Covers 100% of tuition and mandatory fees for IL residents with family AGI <$75,000.

ap_policy.general_rule: There is no total limit for AP credit. Credit is awarded based on specific test scores, generally requiring a 3, 4, or 5 depending on the department.
exceptions: The final 45 credit hours of any degree program must be completed in residence at Illinois Tech.
ap_policy.usage: Can be used to bypass lower-division prerequisites or applied as free electives.

ib_policy.general_rule: College credit is awarded for Higher Level (HL) exams with a score of 4 or better. A maximum of 10 semester hours can be awarded per HL exam.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Maximum of 68 applicable credit hours from a two-year college. Transfer credit requires a grade of 'C' or better (C- is not accepted). Final 45 semester hours must be taken at Illinois Tech.

what_it_takes: Rigorous high school preparation in math and physics.
Passion for hands-on creation, building, and coding.
Self-driven motivation in a relatively quiet social setting.
essay_tips: Focus on tangible projects or research you've engaged in.
Explain specifically why Illinois Tech's hands-on approach (Elevate/IPRO) fits your goals.
red_flags: Expecting a massive SEC-style sports and party culture.
Lack of advanced quantitative coursework on transcripts.
insights: Students consistently highlight the strength of the academics and the high return on investment, but warn that the campus dining and housing facilities can feel dated.
The location in Chicago is praised for networking and internships, but the campus itself is heavily academically focused with limited campus life compared to larger state schools.
